# Figure description: скв_136_и_усл_обозн.jpg

**Source**: `36572_Smolianova_1984/raw_graphics/Колонки_ скважин/скв_136_и_усл_обозн.jpg`

---

## Figure Type

This is a **borehole/well log (каротажная диаграмма / геолого-технический разрез)** — a composite well log or drilling log strip chart. It appears to be a multi-track geophysical well log recorded on a long continuous paper strip, typical of Soviet-era borehole logging practice. The document spans the full vertical depth of a drilled borehole and includes multiple geophysical parameter curves plotted side by side.

---

## Coverage and Subject

The log appears to cover a **single deep borehole** drilled as part of the regional survey described in Report 36572. The header (partially legible) references **КАРОТАЖНАЯ ДИАГРАММА** (Karotazhnaya Diagramma — "Well Log Diagram"), consistent with Soviet borehole geophysical logging. The document is likely associated with one of the parametric or structural boreholes drilled in the northern Kazakhstan survey area (map sheets **M-42** or **N-42**). The depth range appears to extend from near-surface down to approximately **several hundred to possibly 1,000+ metres**, based on the physical length of the log strip. Specific borehole number (скважина №) is noted in the header but is not clearly legible at this image resolution.

---

## Key Content

The log contains **multiple vertical tracks** running the full depth of the borehole, each representing a different geophysical or lithological parameter:

- **Left margin columns**: Likely include depth scale (in metres), lithological description column, and formation/stratigraphic designation columns. Rows appear to be marked at regular depth intervals.
- **Central wiggle-trace curves**: These are the primary geophysical log curves, showing significant variability with depth. The curves display:
  - High-amplitude, rapidly fluctuating sections in the **upper-to-middle portions** of the log — suggesting lithological heterogeneity, possibly fractured or interbedded sequences.
  - More subdued, lower-amplitude fluctuations in the **lower sections** — potentially indicating more homogeneous rock (e.g., basement or massive igneous/metamorphic units).
  - Several prominent **spike anomalies** (sharp excursions) likely indicating resistive or radioactive horizons.
- **Right-side columns**: Appear to contain tabular data, possibly core recovery percentages, RQD values, drilling rate, or mud parameters — standard components of Soviet geotechnical logs.
- **Lower section**: Contains a **legend/explanation block** (легенда) and possibly summary stratigraphic or lithological columns, along with a separate smaller graph at the very bottom (possibly a deviation survey or summary curve).
- The log appears to include at minimum **2–3 geophysical curve types**, which in Soviet practice typically would be: **КС (кажущееся сопротивление / apparent resistivity)**, **ПС (потенциал самополяризации / spontaneous potential)**, and possibly **ГК (гамма-каротаж / gamma-ray log)** or **НГК (нейтронный гамма-каротаж / neutron-gamma log)**.

---

## Text and Labels

Due to image resolution limitations, full transcription is constrained, but the following elements are visible or inferable:

- **Title area (top centre)**: **КАРОТАЖНАЯ ДИАГРАММА** — "Well Log Diagram"
- **Header fields** (standard Soviet log header format) likely include:
  - Скважина № (Borehole No.)
  - Площадь / Участок (Area / Site)
  - Организация (Organization)
  - Масштаб глубин (Depth scale) — likely **1:500** or **1:200**
  - Дата (Date)
- **Left depth column**: Tick marks at regular intervals, likely every **10 m**
- **Curve labels** (partially visible at top of tracks): Possibly **КС**, **ПС**, **ГК**
- **Bottom legend block**: Contains lithological symbols and formation descriptions — includes hatched, dotted, and lined symbols typical of Soviet stratigraphic legend conventions
- **Bottom right**: Appears to contain a summary table with formation tops, depths, and possibly core sample data
- **Annotation near middle of log**: Some handwritten or printed depth callouts and formation boundary lines crossing the tracks

## Research Value

This well log provides **direct subsurface lithological and petrophysical data** for a specific borehole location within the surveyed area, documenting how resistivity, spontaneous potential, and/or radioactivity vary with depth — which is essential for correlating surface geophysical anomalies (gravity, magnetic, seismic) with actual rock units encountered in drilling. A geologist or AI research assistant could use this log to **identify formation boundaries, detect mineralized or altered horizons** (e.g., zones of anomalous resistivity or elevated gamma response indicative of ore-bearing or hydrothermally altered rock), and **calibrate the regional geophysical models** constructed from the M-42/N-42 survey. In the context of mineral exploration in northern Kazakhstan, this log would be critical for evaluating the depth, thickness, and character of prospective units identified by surface surveys.
